sections.summary: Document title only: Standard for Pearl Millet Flour (not a substantive regulatory provision).
Standard identification code CXS 170‑1989 (document identifier, not substantive).

sections.section: Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Provides the adoption and amendment history: adopted in 1989, revised in 1995, and amended in 2019 and 2023.

Applies to pearl millet flour for direct human consumption obtained from Pennisetum americanum L., including Senegalese varieties 'souna' and 'sanio', and expressly excludes grits or coarse grain from pearl millet.

Defines pearl millet flour as the product for human consumption obtained by industrial milling of pearl millet grains with substantial removal of the germ and reduction of the endosperm to a fine powder.

Requires pearl millet flour to be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours, odours, living insects and filth that may pose a health hazard; sets a maximum moisture content of 13% m/m and allows lower moisture limits for certain destinations depending on climate, transport and storage.

General quality requirements for pearl millet flour: it must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours and living insects, and free from animal-origin filth (including dead insects) in amounts that may pose a health hazard.

sections.section: 3.1.1 Pearl millet flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Pearl millet flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Pearl millet flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
sections.summary: Pearl millet flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Pearl millet flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origins, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
sections.summary: Pearl millet flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Section heading for specific quality factors for pearl millet flour; no substantive provisions are provided under this heading in the text.

Sets a maximum moisture content for pearl millet flour at 13% m/m and notes that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations depending on climate and duration of transport and storage.

sections.section: Lower  moisture  limits  should  be  required  for  certain  destinations  in  relation  to  the  climate,  duration  of transport and storage.
sections.summary: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations based on climate and the expected duration of transport and storage.

Contaminants provisions for pearl millet flour: it must be free from heavy metals in hazardous amounts and must comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum residue limits for pesticide residues and mycotoxins for this commodity.
Pearl millet flour shall be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Pearl millet flour shall comply with maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.
Pearl millet flour shall comply with maximum mycotoxin limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.

Hygiene requirements recommend preparing and handling pearl millet flour per the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and related CAC codes, require the product to be free from objectionable matter under good manufacturing practice, and specify that when tested it must not contain microorganisms, parasites, or substances of microbial origin in amounts that may present a health hazard.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 1  and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: Recommendation that pearl millet flour be prepared and handled in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant CAC codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: To the extent consistent with good manufacturing practice, pearl millet flour shall be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.summary: When tested by appropriate sampling and examination, pearl millet flour shall be free from microorganisms and parasites in amounts that may represent a health hazard and shall not contain substances originating from microorganisms in hazardous amounts.

Packaging must safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities of pearl millet flour; containers and packaging materials must be safe for intended use and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour/flavour, and sacks (when used) must be clean, sturdy and securely sewn or sealed.

sections.section: 6.1 Pearl millet flour shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Pearl millet flour shall be packaged in containers that safeguard its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Containers and packaging materials must be made of substances safe and suitable for their intended use and must not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: When packaged in sacks, the sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

Labelling heading states that, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), specific provisions apply, but no specific labelling provisions are included in the provided text.
Requires the product name shown on the label to be "pearl millet flour."
Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard shall be those contained in the Recommended methods of analysis and sampling (CXS 234-1999) for checking compliance.

Advises users to specify the appropriate factor limit and method where multiple limits/methods are given, and sets compositional and particle-size requirements for pearl millet flour: fine flour 100% passing 0.5 mm sieve, medium flour 100% passing 1 mm sieve; ash 0.8–1.0% (dry matter), protein (N×5.7) ≥8.0% (dry matter), fat ≤5.0% (dry matter), crude fibre ≤1.5% (dry matter), colour 18–30 Kent‑Jones units, and food additives must conform to the legislation of the country of sale (methods referenced to Section 8 or none defined where noted).
Non‑substantive bibliographic notes listing referenced Codex documents (CXC 1-1969; CXS 1-1985; CXS 234-1999).
